prompt:
Branch ops report, electrical wholesale distribution. Since the intake/picking rework went out, Monday reconciliation is a disaster in three distinct ways. I've pulled the paperwork and re-done the math by hand for each one; the numbers below are exact.

1) Orders that come in through QUOTE CONVERSION bill low, every time. Halvorsen Electric is on a 12% contract. Their standing basket is 4 spools of 12 AWG THHN at $89.50 list and 40 sticks of 3/4-in EMT at $6.85 list. Keyed in directly at the desk that bills $556.16, which is right: 358.00 less 12% is 315.04, 274.00 less 12% is 241.12. The identical basket converted from the rep's quote Q-7741 billed $489.50. Work that backwards and it's the 12% coming off twice — list accounts (no contract percentage) convert from quotes and bill perfectly, so it's specifically contract accounts through the quote path.

2) Our pick carts hold 12 totes, hard limit — one line per tote. Since the rework, pickers keep photographing 13-line cart tickets, and a 26-line morning that should cut 12/12/2 came out as two 13-line tickets instead of three carts. Line 13 has no tote, it rides loose on the cart, and twice now it's been the line that went missing.

3) Same part, picked and shipped twice. Two of our customers' punchout systems disagree on SKU casing — one sends BRK-220-2P, the other brk-220-2p. Both are supposed to land on ONE order line (SKU matching has always been case-insensitive here — catalog lookup already treats them as the same part). Friday a PO carrying the breaker under both casings produced two separate pick lines, 4 and 2, and the customer got billed for a 6-pack split across two cartons with two pick tickets. Should have been one merged line of 6.

`dotnet test` reproduces all three (plus the behaviors that already work and must not break). Please root-cause and fix the services — could be one change or several, I honestly can't tell from the floor. Do not touch FulfillmentTests.cs, WireDepot.csproj, or .gitignore.
